Petition For Approval Of Compromise Of Claim
Off Calendar Sep-13-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar for motion of compromise of claim for a person with a disability.

Dept: 505; Judge: Michelle Tong; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceedings reported by "For the Record" only to be used in compliance with California Government Code Section 69957, commencing at 9:23:33 a.m. and concluding at 9:33:12 a.m. (D505).

Jason Cirlin, Esq. representing Petitioner Richard Dingman who was placed under oath via Zoom Video Conference.
David Henshaw, Esq. representing Defendant William Ellis as an observer.

Counsel to update the court as to the status of the case.
The request for the compromise of claim is discussed.
The Court examines Petitioner Guardian Ad litem. Petitioner Guardian ad litem testifies.
Petitioner Guardian ad litem confirms that the settlement is in the best interest of Paul Langley and Paul Langley Company, A California Corporation and confirms as to the court's examination.

The Court grants the request for compromise of claim for Paul Langley and Paul Langley Company, A California Corporation. See scanned order.

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Apr-24-2024 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-04-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF PAUL LANGLEY COMPANY, PAUL LANGLEY's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion for leave to file first amended complaint (FAC) is granted. "That trial courts are to liberally permit such amendments, at any stage of the proceeding, has been established policy in this state since 1901." (Hirsa v. Sup, Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89; see also CCP 428.50(c).)

The FAC adds no new facts and one cause of action. The motion is opposed only by defendant William Ellis - and belatedly so. If counsel act expeditiously, the May 20 trial date need not be continued.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
